title: setaria pumila subsp. subtesselata
intro: Setaria pumila subsp. subtesselata, also known as yellow foxtail, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to Africa, Asia, and Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of yellow foxtail include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. Yellow foxtail can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
